topic: Product workflow writes prepared through the operation registry
context: v1.2 Phase 5 refactors product workflows internally only where operation-registry reuse removes duplicate command/snapshot/restore logic without changing public workflow behavior.
observation: Routing Review route writes and mixer bus renames now use operations.prepare_operation(...).safe_write_group_entry() before safety.safe_write_group. Mix Review trim_volume now prepares mixer.set_volume through the registry before safety.safe_write. Persistent fl_batch uses the same helper for grouped write entries.
tested_values: Mix Review trim_volume track 4 target -3.0 dB
persistent fl_batch mixer mute write tests
operation registry mixer volume helper tests
result: Registry-prepared route entries preserve command and restore payloads while adding explicit route readback verification. Registry-prepared mixer name and Mix Review volume writes preserve previous command/scope/restore behavior. Public FastMCP registration does not change.
source_method: Focused offline pytest tests with fake bridges plus static safety audit.
known_pitfalls: Project Organizer channel rename helpers were not refactored because channel name registry validation requires non-empty names.
Project Organizer hex color helpers were not refactored because current registry color specs accept FL-native integer colors or RGB triplets, not hex strings.
open_questions: Live FL Studio smoke tests were not run for the refactored product workflow paths.
Project Organizer color input compatibility should be handled in a separate slice if that public behavior is corrected.
next_recommended_action: Keep product workflow writes aligned with the operation registry while preserving public workflow behavior.
